'''Ryan Hoffman''' (born 26 January 1984) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=PlayersVoice|title=Silencing the inner voice|url=https://www.playersvoice.com.au/cameron-smith-silencing-the-inner-voice|access-date=2008-10-06|date=11 March 2019}}</ref> He played for [[Melbourne Storm]] and the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], as a {{rlp|sr}}.

==Early life==
Hoffman was born in [[Canberra]], [[Australian Capital Territory]]. His father, [[Jay Hoffman (rugby league)|Jay Hoffman]], played professional football for the [[Canberra Raiders]] with Ryan’s future [[Melbourne Storm]] coach [[Craig Bellamy]], representing [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] in the pre-[[State of Origin series|Origin]] era.<ref>{{cite web|website=Queensland Rugby League|title=Origin teams for game two|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/display.php?pg_id=3009|access-date=2007-10-30|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070904073622/http://www.qrl.com.au/display.php?pg_id=3009|archive-date=4 September 2007}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=So it is a family game, after all|url=http://www.smh.com.au/articles/2004/04/16/1082055656494.html?from=storyrhs|access-date=2008-10-06|date=17 April 2004}}</ref>

Hoffman attended the renowned rugby league school{{citation needed|date=January 2025}} [[St Gregory's College, Campbelltown|St Gregory's College]] in [[Campbelltown, New South Wales]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.camdenadvertiser.com.au/story/241366/school-of-nrl-success/|title=School of NRL success|publisher=Camden-Narellan Advertiser|date=13 March 2012|access-date=29 December 2015}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Melbourne Storm (2003–2010)===
[[File:Clint Newton and Ryan Hoffman in 2007.JPG|250px|thumb|left|Hoffman and [[Clint Newton]] in 2007 celebrating after a win for Melbourne]]
Hoffman started his NRL career with the [[Melbourne Storm]] in 2003. In 2006, his improving form led to him being selected for the [[New South Wales City rugby league team|New South Wales City Origin side]] for the first time. It also earned him the Storm's ''Forward of the Year'' award. In addition, he was named in the second row for Melbourne Storm's team of the decade.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2013/11/12/team-of-the-decade.html|title=Team of the Decade|publisher=Melbourne Storm official site|date=13 November 2012|access-date=29 December 2015}}</ref>

Following the [[2006 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]], Hoffman sat in the middle of [[Telstra Stadium]] in tears, an image which was printed in newspapers all around Australia.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.australiantimes.co.uk/champion-of-champions/|title=Champion of champions|publisher=australiantimes.com.au|date=12 February 2010|access-date=29 December 2015}}</ref>

In 2007, Hoffman was part of the Melbourne team which won the [[National Rugby League season 2007#Grand final|Grand Final]], defeating [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] by 34–8. He played in the [[2009 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[Parramatta Eels]], scoring the opening try.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-04/nrl-grand-final/1091482|title=NRL grand final|publisher=ABC News|date=4 September 2009|access-date=29 December 2015}}</ref> In 2010, both premierships which Hoffman won with Melbourne were stripped due to the club's [[Melbourne Storm salary cap breach|breaches of the salary cap between 2006 and 2010]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/storm/they-are-ours-why-cameron-smith-will-always-recognise-storms-stripped-premierships/news-story/6397becb6e5091d6804ef91c578c56bd/|title='They are ours': Why Cameron Smith will always recognise Storm's stripped premierships|publisher=Fox Sports|date=31 July 2019}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors (2011)===
In July 2010, Hoffman signed with [[Super League]] club [[Wigan Warriors]], where he was given the #16 shirt and replaced [[Phil Bailey]] at second-row.<ref>{{cite web|last=Lucius|first=Adam|title=Storm lose rep pair|url=http://www.sportal.com.au/league-news-display/storm-lose-rep-pair-94766|work=Sportal Australia|access-date=5 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Warriors Announce 2011 Squad Numbers|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=5431&type=1|work=Wigan Warriors|access-date=5 May 2011}}</ref>

Hoffman scored on his first-grade début for the side at the season-opening [[Magic Weekend]] event against the club's rivals, [[St Helens R.F.C.]]<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9393298.stm|title=St Helens 16-16 Wigan|date=12 February 2011}}</ref>

He then featured in the [[2011 World Club Challenge]] where Wigan lost 15–21 to [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in their first home game of the season.<ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan Warriors 15-21 St George Illawarra Dragons|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/9403491.stm|access-date=5 May 2011|publisher=BBC|date=27 February 2011}}</ref>

In rounds 4 and 5, Hoffman scored a try apiece against [[Hull F.C.]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]] respectively.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9417484.stm|title=Wigan 14-12 Hull FC|date=13 March 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Stewart|first=Rob|title=Huddersfield Giants 6 Wigan Warriors 20: match report|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8392044/Huddersfield-Giants-6-Wigan-Warriors-20-match-report.html|access-date=5 May 2011|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|date=18 March 2011}}</ref> Another try at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in Round 9 made his record four tries from ten starting games for Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull KR 16-28 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9455872.stm|access-date=5 May 2011|publisher=BBC|date=15 April 2011}}</ref>

Hoffman played in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]], playing as a second-row forward, helping the side to defeat Leeds 28–18.<ref>{{cite news|last=wiganwarriors.com|title=Wigan are 2011 Cup Champions|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40914|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=rleague.com|date=28 August 2011|archive-date=20 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120620135402/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40914|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=therfl.co.uk|title=Challenge Cup: Lima inspires Wigan win|url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40911|access-date=26 August 2012|newspaper=rleague.com|date=28 August 2011|archive-date=20 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120620125551/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40911|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Chisnall|first=Craig|title=Lima double inspires Wigan to Cup win|url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8291057|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[Wide World of Sports (Australian TV series)|Wide World of Sports]]|date=28 August 2011|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121230192159/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8291057|archive-date=30 December 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Melbourne Storm (2012–2014)===
Hoffman signed a new deal to return to the NRL and the Melbourne Storm in 2012. Midseason, he captained the team for four games in the absence of [[Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)|Cameron Smith]], becoming the 18th player to captain the club.{{cn|date=June 2025}} He played in their [[2012 NRL Grand Final|2012 Premiership]] victory over [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]], scoring the first try of the game.

He set up the first try for teammate [[Billy Slater]] in the [[2013 World Club Challenge]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8615807 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130226084200/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8615807 |archive-date=26 February 2013 |title=Storm beat Leeds in World Club Challenge}}</ref>

===New Zealand Warriors (2015–2017)===
On 7 May 2014, Hoffman announced that he had signed a three-year deal with the [[New Zealand Warriors]], worth an estimated $1.6 million.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10019629/Storms-Ryan-Hoffman-to-sign-for-NZ-Warriors|title=Storm's Ryan Hoffman to sign for NZ Warriors|publisher=stuff.co.nz|date=7 May 2014|access-date=29 December 2015}}</ref>

In April 2015, Hoffman played his 250th NRL career game against his former club Melbourne Storm at [[AAMI Park]].

On 17 February 2016, Hoffman was named the new club captain, after incumbent captain [[Simon Mannering]] stepped down from the role.{{cn|date=June 2025}} Hoffman is the second Australian to have captained the New Zealand club, after [[Steve Price (rugby league)|Steve Price]].

On 1 February 2017, new Warriors coach [[Stephen Kearney]] announced that Hoffman would be replaced as captain and would not be re-signed for the 2018 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=11793045|title=Warriors coach Stephen Kearney confirms Ryan Hoffman exit in 2018}}</ref>

On 10 June, Hoffman became just the 27th player (in the game) to reach the 300th game milestone. In his 300th game, he scored the first try of the game (in the first half) and kicked a conversion goal (to Hingano's try, in the 80th minute) during the [[New Zealand Warriors|Warriors]]' 34-12 round 14 win against the [[Gold Coast Titans]].

===Melbourne Storm (2018)===
On 10 October 2017, the Melbourne Storm announced that Hoffman would return to the club on a 1-year deal that saw him end his playing career at the club. On 3 July 2018, Hoffman publicly announced that he was retiring at the end of the [[2018 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite news| title=Hoffman signs with Storm| website=Heraldsun.com.au | date=30 November 2021 | url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/melbourne-storm-champion-ryan-hoffman-set-to-return-for-nrl-swan-song/news-story/1759e1c5ec59b04510ffdaf7cf4aedfa | access-date=15 May 2023}} (subscription required)</ref>

==Representative career==
[[File:Ryan Hoffmann.JPG|thumb|Hoffman playing for Australia]]
While attending Campbelltown St. Gregory's College, Hoffman played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 2002.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=2008-10-10| publisher=SportingPulse}}</ref>

He was named as the [[List of rugby league terms|18th man]] for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the 2007 [[ANZAC Test]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. In October, 2007, he was selected in the run-on side for the Centenary Test against New Zealand in Wellington.<ref>{{cite web |title=New Zealand vs Australia 2007 |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/new-zealand-vs-australia-2007/game-1/new-zealand-vs-australia.html |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=25 October 2022}}</ref>

Again as 18th man, Hoffman was on stand-by for the 2007 [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] State of Origin Team in game 1 of the series. He was selected on the bench in game 2 and 3 of the same series.

In 2008, Hoffman was named in the New South Wales starting side in [[2008 State of Origin series|all three Origin matches]].

In August 2008, he was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team]] preliminary 46-man Kangaroos squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|author=Liam FitzGibbon|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|title=Surprises in Kangaroos squad|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24111780-23214,00.html|access-date=2008-08-02|date=1 August 2008}}</ref><ref name="Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad">{{cite news|url=http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|title=Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad|newspaper=International Herald Tribune|date=1 August 2008|access-date=2008-08-02}}</ref>

He was selected for City in the [[City vs Country Origin|City vs Country]] match on 8 May 2009.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|title=Blues halfback duel in two as Mitchell Pearce misses selection|first=Todd|last=Balym|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|date=3 May 2009|access-date=2009-05-03}}</ref>
